steps.title: Test the feasibility claim
steps.content: Benz and colleagues conclude that if surface temperature approaches the maximum plausible level, more sites could use subsurface thermal pollution to help meet home heating needs. The supporting data should therefore show a shift from sites with no usable contribution toward sites with at least some usable contribution.

steps.title: Read the zero-use change
steps.content: Choice A invents a category in which all heating needs are met, but the graph groups sites as 0%, up to 25%, or more than 25%. Choice B confuses the potential to use thermal pollution with having no need for heating. Choice C misstates the current-temperature data because more than 80% of sites are in the 0% group, not the up-to-25% group. The drop from more than 80% unable to use any thermal pollution to below 20% at the maximum plausible temperature directly shows that feasible sites increase. The answer is D.
